subject: Science
text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants make food using sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water. It produces glucose and releases oxygen.

subject: Science
text: Plants use chlorophyll to capture sunlight energy. This energy helps convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose (food).

subject: Science
text: In photosynthesis, inputs are sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide (CO₂). Outputs are glucose (food) and oxygen.

subject: Science
text: Respiration is the process by which living cells release energy from food. Cells use oxygen to break down glucose and release energy.

subject: Science
text: Plant respiration happens day and night. It uses oxygen and glucose and produces carbon dioxide, water, and energy.

subject: Science
text: Gravity is the force that attracts objects toward the Earth. It is why objects fall when dropped.

subject: Science
text: Gravity acts on objects with mass. The gravitational force increases when mass increases and decreases with distance.

subject: Science
text: A force is a push or pull that can change an object's motion. Force can start motion, stop motion, or change direction.

subject: Science
text: Motion describes a change in position over time. Speed is distance divided by time, and acceleration is change in velocity over time.

subject: Science
text: An atom is the smallest unit of an element. A molecule is formed when two or more atoms join together.

subject: Science
text: Elements are made of one type of atom. Compounds are made when atoms of different elements combine in fixed ratios.
